volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: Amphitryon (Section 2.2.778c - Section 2.2.825)

sections.label: Section 2.2.778c
sections.translation: Come , now then , look here , if you please , you who deny what is fact , and whom I shall now clearly convict in this case .

sections.label: Section 2.2.780
sections.original: estne haec patera , qua donatu'sdonatus es illi ?
sections.translation: Isn ’ t this the goblet with which you were presented there ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.780b
sections.translation: Supreme Jupiter ! what do I behold ? Surely this is that goblet . Sosia , I ’ m utterly confounded .

sections.label: Section 2.2.782
sections.original: Aut pol haec praestigiatrix multo mulier maxima est
sections.translation: Upon my faith , either this woman is a most consummate juggler , or the goblet must be in here ( pointing to the casket .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.783b
sections.translation: Come , then , open this casket .

sections.label: Section 2.2.784
sections.original: Quid ego istam exsolvam ? obsignatast recte , res gesta est bene :
sections.translation: Why should I open it ? It is securely sealed . The thing is cleverly contrived ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.785
sections.original: tu peperisti Ámphitruonem , ego alium peperi Sosiam ;
sections.translation: you have brought forth another Amphitryon , I have brought forth another Sosia ; now if the goblet has brought forth a goblet , we have all produced our doubles .

sections.label: Section 2.2.787
sections.translation: I ’ m determined to open and examine it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.787b
sections.translation: Look , please , how the seal is , that you may not hereafter throw the blame on me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.788b
sections.translation: Now do open it . For she certainly is desirous to drive us mad with her talking .

sections.label: Section 2.2.790
sections.original: Vnde haec igitur est nisi abs te quae mihi dono data est ?
sections.translation: Whence then came this which was made a present to me , but from yourself ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.791
sections.translation: It ’ s necessary for me to enquire into this .

sections.label: Section 2.2.791b
sections.translation: Jupiter , O Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 2.2.792
sections.translation: What is the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.792b
sections.translation: There ’ s no goblet here in the casket .

sections.label: Section 2.2.792c
sections.translation: What do I hear .

sections.label: Section 2.2.793
sections.translation: That which is the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.2.793b
sections.original: At cum cruciatu iam , nisi apparet , tuo .
sections.translation: But at your peril now , if it does not make its appearance .

sections.label: Section 2.2.794
sections.translation: Why , it does make its appearance .

sections.label: Section 2.2.794b
sections.translation: Who then gave it you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.794c
sections.translation: The person that ’ s asking me the question .

sections.label: Section 2.2.795
sections.original: Me captas , quia tute ab navi clanculum huc alia via
sections.translation: You are on the catch for me , inasmuch as you yourself have secretly run before me hither from the ship by another road , and have taken the goblet away from here and given it to her , and afterwards you have secretly sealed it up again .

sections.label: Section 2.2.798
sections.original: Ei mihi , iam tu quoque huius adiuvas insaniam ?
sections.translation: Ah me ! and are you too helping her frenzy as well ? ( To ALCMENA ) Do you say that we arrived here yesterday ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.799b
sections.translation: I do say so , and on your arrival you instantly

sections.label: Section 2.2.800
sections.original: me salutavisti , et ego te , et osculum tetuli tibi .
sections.translation: greeted me , and I you , and I gave you a kiss .

sections.label: Section 2.2.801
sections.original: Iam illud non placet principium de osculo .
sections.translation: That beginning now about the kiss doesn ’ t please me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.801b
sections.translation: Go on telling it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.802
sections.translation: Then you bathed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.802b
sections.translation: What , after I bathed ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.802c
sections.translation: You took your place at table .

sections.label: Section 2.2.802d
sections.translation: Bravo , capital ! Now make further enquiry .

sections.label: Section 2.2.803b
sections.translation: Don ’ t you interrupt . ( To ALCMENA .) Go on telling me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.804
sections.original: Cena adposita est ; cenavisti mecum , ego accubui simul .
sections.translation: The dinner was served ; you dined with me ; I reclined together with you at the repast .

sections.label: Section 2.2.805
sections.translation: What , on the same couch ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.805b
sections.translation: On the same .

sections.label: Section 2.2.805c
sections.translation: Oh dear , I don ’ t like this banquet .

sections.label: Section 2.2.806
sections.original: Sine modo argumenta dicat . quid postquam cenavimus ?
sections.translation: Now do let her give her proofs . ( To ALCMENA .) What , after we had dined ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.807
sections.original: Te dormitare aibas ; mensa ablata est , cubitum hinc abiimus .
sections.translation: You said that you were inclined to go to sleep ; the table was removed ; thence we went to bed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.808
sections.translation: Where did you lie ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.808b
sections.original: In eodem lectó tecum una in cubiculo .
sections.translation: In the chamber , in the same bed together with your . self .

sections.label: Section 2.2.809
sections.translation: You have proved my undoing .

sections.label: Section 2.2.809b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.809c
sections.original: Haec me modo ad mortem dedit .
sections.translation: This very moment has she sent me to my grave .

sections.label: Section 2.2.810
sections.translation: How so , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.810b
sections.translation: Don ’ t address me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.810c
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.810d
sections.translation: To my sorrow I ’ m undone , since , in my absence from here , dishonor has befallen her chastity .

sections.label: Section 2.2.812
sections.original: Obsecro ecastor , cur istuc , mi vir , ex ted audio ?
sections.translation: In heaven ’ s name , my lord , why , I beseech you , do I hear this from you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.813
sections.original: Vir ego tuos sim ? ne me appella , falsa , falso nomine .
sections.translation: I , your lord ? False one , don ’ t call me by a false name .

sections.label: Section 2.2.814
sections.original: Haeret haec res , si quidem haec iam mulier facta est ex viro .
sections.translation: ’ Tis an odd matter [*] this , if indeed he has been made into my lady from my lord .

sections.label: Section 2.2.815
sections.original: Quid ego feci , qua istaec propter dicta dicantur mihi ?
sections.translation: What have I done , by reason of which these expressions are uttered to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.816
sections.original: Tute edictas facta tua , ex me quaeris quid deliqueris .
sections.translation: You yourself proclaim your own doings ; do you enquire of me in what you have offended ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.817
sections.original: Quid ego tibi deliqui , si , cui nupta sum , tecum fui ?
sections.translation: In what have I offended you , if I have been with you to whom I am married ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.818
sections.original: Tun mecum fueris ? quid illac impudente audacius ?
sections.translation: You , been with me ? What is there of greater effrontery than this impudent woman ? At least , if you were wanting in modesty of your own , you might have borrowed it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.820
sections.original: Istuc facinus , quod tu insimulas , nostro generi non decet .
sections.translation: That criminality which you lay to my charge befits not my family . If you try to catch me in incontinence , you cannot convict me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.822
sections.original: Pro di immortales , cognoscin tu me saltem , Sosia ?
sections.translation: Immortal Gods ! do you at least know me , Sosia ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.823
sections.translation: Pretty well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.823b
sections.original: Cenavin ego heri in navi in portu Persico ?
sections.translation: Did I not dine yesterday on board ship in the Persian Port ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.824
sections.original: Mihi quoque adsunt testes , qui illud quod ego dicam adsentiant .
sections.translation: I have witnesses as well , who can confirm that which I say .

sections.label: Section 2.2.825
sections.original: Nescio quid istúc negoti dicam , nisi si quispiam est
sections.translation: I don ’ t know what to say to this matter , unless , perchance , there is another Amphitryon , who , perhaps , though you yourself are absent , takes care of your business , and who , in your absence , performs your duties here . For about that counterfeit Sosia it is very surprising . Certainly , about this Amphitryon , now , it is another matter still more surprising .
